title: edatasheets compound schema bundle
$comment: Generated from the local part-spec schema tree.

fixedFrequency.description: clock frequency value if the clock has a fixed frequency

numberClockOutputs.description: number of clock outputs in a clock IC

differentialSingleEnded.description: property describing whether a clock output is single ended or differential

frequencyTolerance.description: amount of frequency variation from nominal frequency

powerSupplyRejectionRatio.description: power supply rejection ratio (PSRR) or ratio between power supply variation and output variation

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes

frequencyStability.description: frequency change over temperature, load, supply voltage change and aging

currentConsumption.description: current consumption of a device

outputLoad.description: maximum capacitive load that can be supported by oscillator

riseTime.description: time for output to go from 10% to 90% of output max

fallTime.description: time for output to go from 90% to 10% of output max

startUpTime.description: time between enable and output reaching 10% of output max

dutyCycle.description: time above 50% of output max over entire period

common__componentID.title: specification to capture information to identify components

componentName.description: base part name that describes the form and fit of a component

orderableMPN.description: orderable manufacturer part numbers, including packing and software information

sourceDatasheetID.description: methods for identifying the human-readable source information for a electronic datasheet

digitalDatasheetID.description: methods for identifying the version of the electronic datasheet

complianceList.description: list of standards the part complies with

publishedDate.description: date the source datasheet was published

datasheetURI.description: uri to the source datasheet pdf or html view

productURI.description: uri to the source datasheet's product page'

publishedDate.description: date the electronic datasheet was published

eDatasheetSpecRevision.description: revision of the electronic datasheet specifications used to create the electronic datasheet

guid.description: vendor defined guid (see https://www.guidgenerator.com/) to uniquely identify electronic datasheet version

common__componentProtectionThresholds.title: specification to capture protection thresholds data of a component

thermalShutdownThresholdRising.description: thermal Shutdown (tsd) Threshold with temperature rising

thermalShutdownThresholdFalling.description: thermal Shutdown (tsd) Threshold with temperature falling

powerSupplyProtection.description: undervoltage lockout, overvoltage protection thresholds of a supply

supplyPinName.description: name of the power supply pin or any other pin monitored

overVoltageProtectionThresholdRising.description: Overvoltage Protection (OVP) Threshold with power supply rising

overVoltageProtectionThresholdFalling.description: Overvoltage Protection (OVP) Threshold with power supply falling

underVoltageLockoutThresholdRising.description: Undervoltage Lockout (UVLO) Threshold with power supply rising

underVoltageLockoutThresholdFalling.description: Undervoltage Lockout (UVLO) Threshold with power supply falling

common__coreProperties.title: reference to all core properties specifications

oneOf.title: usb_bc12
oneOf.description: specification of USB battery charging 1.2 (bc12) detector

common__currentConsumption.title: specification to capture the current consumption data of a component

activeCurrent.description: current consumption when a device is in active mode

sleepCurrent.description: current consumption when a device is in sleep mode

idleCurrent.description: current consumption when a device is in idle mode

common__externalFile.title: specification for referencing an external file

fileDescription.description: text description of the contents of an external file

otherFileType.description: type of file being linked, if not in the standard list

companionSoftware.description: optional, name of software program used to access file

standardReferenced.description: optional, name of the standard the file is written in

fileURI.description: URI linking to the CAD file
examples: could be a URL or file path with filename

common__externalFileMap.title: specification for referencing an external file for different components

coreProperties.description: core component properties as defined by the specific component spec file. These properties are described by the common part of the part number
examples: this might include everything about an MCU other than flash size

additionalCoreProperties.description: core component properties as defined by the specific component spec file. These properties are described by the changing part of the part number
examples: this might include the MCU flash size

pins.description: pin properties specified by the pin spec file

package.description: package information specified by the package spec file

thermal.description: component temperature and thermal resistance information

numberOfCurves.description: total number of curves in graph

curve.description: data represented by one or more curves in a graph

label.description: description of the data captured by a curve

common__instanceSpec.title: specification of an instance in a part

partName.description: name of the part defined in the electronic datasheets specifications

instanceName.description: name of an instance of the part

instanceProperties.description: instance properties, as defined in the part specification

length.description: length of a side of a package

width.description: width of a side of a package

common__pinPaths.title: specification of pinpaths through an ic

numberOfPinPaths.description: number of pinPaths defined. This number should not be higher than the number of components included in the part

partPinPaths.description: list of pins associated with each component in a multi-component part
examples: for a dual FET part with components FET1 and FET2: list of pins associated with FET1 and list of pins associated with FET2

componentName.description: name of a component within a part

componentPinNames.description: names of pins associated with each of the component in the part. Pin names must match the name used in part pin definition

terminalIdentifier.description: pin or ball number(s) as defined by datasheet

name.description: name given to the signal appearing at the terminal of a component

description.description: description of the signal appearing at the terminal of an electric/electronic component

numberOfSupportedFunctions.description: the total number of functions supported by this pin

pinProperties.description: list of properties for each pin

functionProperties.description: list of properties for each pin function configuration

pinPaths.description: information on pin paths - pins associated with each component in a multi-component part (such as A1,Y1 and A2,Y2)

perFunctionName.description: name of the function of a pin

perFunctionProperties.description: list of pin properties that change based on the pin function configuration

vih.description: the high-level input voltage for which operation of the logic element within specification limits is to be expected

vil.description: the low-level input voltage for which operation of the logic element within specification limits is to be expected

vol.description: the voltage level at an output terminal with input conditions applied that, according to the product specification, will establish a low level at the output

voh.description: the voltage level at an output terminal with input conditions applied that, according to the product specification, will establish a high level at the output

absVmax.description: maximum voltage rating beyond which damage to the device may occur
absVmax.$comment: units of volts, if this voltage is between two pins, make a note in the conditions field

absVmin.description: absolute minimum voltage that can be applied to a pin

voltageOperatingRange.description: voltage operating range that can safely be applied to a pin

currentRange.description: current range that can safely be drawn/injected from/to a pin

inputLeakage.description: current draw out of a high impedance input pin

outputLeakage.description: current flow from a pin during the off state

dcResistance.description: resistance of a pin of a connector

interfaceType.description: type of interface enabled by pin

polarity.description: whether the active state of a pin is high or low

voltageOptions.description: list of voltage levels supported by a pin

floatUnused.description: description of whether pin can safely be floated if it is not used

internalPullUp.description: indicates the value of an internal pull-up on a pin

internalPullDown.description: indicates the value of an internal pull-down on a pin

esd.description: indicates whether ESD protection exists on a pin

externalComponents.description: list of external component structures recommended to be attached to a pin

componentType.description: type of external component required to be connected to a pin

configuration.description: electrical configuration of component connected to pin with respect to the pin

singlePowerFetPair.description: single pair of power fets (typical in buck or boost configuration)

inputPowerFetPair.description: input power fet pair (in a buck-boost configuration)

outputPowerFetPair.description: output power fet pair (in a buck-boost configuration)

ilimHSFET.description: maximum sustained output current under which the high side FET will operate properly

ilimLSFET.description: maximum sustained output current under which the low side FET will operate properly

powerTransitionName.description: datasheet specific name given to a particular power transition for tracking

signal1.description: signal that comes up first in the sequence. Also used if there is only one relevant signal for a power transition (such as rise time)

signal1TerminalIdentifiers.description: list of component pins associated with signal 1

signal2.description: signal that comes up second in the sequence

signal2TerminalIdentifiers.description: list of component pins associated with signal 2

timeCondition.description: time between the signal 1 and signal 2 events

powerDirection.description: whether this is a power up or power down event

nextPowerState.description: power state the device enters after the signal transition

currentPowerState.description: power state the device is currently in before the signal transition

riseTime.description: time for a signal to go from low to high (only applies to one signal)

slewRate.description: maximum rate at which a voltage rail changes per time (only applies to one signal)

fallTime.description: time for a signal to go from high to low (only applies to one signal)

transitionStartCondition.description: the percentage of max voltage where rise or fall time starts being measured for timing (only applies to one signal)

transitionEndCondition.description: the percentage of max voltage where rise or fall time stops being measured for timing (only applies to one signal)

registerEndianness.description: memory storage order for the bytes

registerIpName.description: name of the IP or interface controlled by changes in the register

registerBitField.description: describes the bit fields in the register

bitFieldLongName.description: Long Name of the bit field

bitFieldResetValue.description: Reset value of a bit field

bitFieldAccessType.description: Access type of a bit field

common__reliability.title: specification of the reliability of a component

failuresInTime.description: the number of expected failures per one billion hours of operation for a device
failuresInTime.$comment: expressed as a number with conditions on how FIT is measured

meanTimeToFail.description: the average time a product or system functions before its first failure under normal conditions

common__thermal.title: specification of thermal properties on components

junctionTemperature.description: recommended operating junction temperature range of a part

junctionTemperatureAbsMax.description: absolute maximum junction temperature of a part

ambientTemperature.description: recommended operating ambient air temperature range of a part

ambientTemperatureAbsMax.description: absolute maximum ambient air temperature of a part

caseTemperature.description: case temperature range of a part

caseTemperatureAbsMax.description: absolute maximum case temperature of a part

leadTemperatureAbsMax.description: absolute maximum lead temperature of a part

storageTemperatureAbsMax.description: absolute maximum storage temperature of a part

packageThermalResistance.description: package thermal resistance of a part

thermalResistanceJunctionToAmbient.description: thermal resistance between the semiconductor junction of a part and the ambient air

thermalResistanceJunctionToCase.description: thermal resistance between the semiconductor junction and the package surface of a part

thermalResistanceJunctionToBoard.description: thermal resistance between the semiconduction junction of a part and the board

thermalResistanceJunctionToLead.description: thermal resistance between the semiconduction junction of a part and the solder pad

thermalResistanceCaseToAmbient.description: thermal resistance between the package surface of a part and the ambient air

thermalDesignPower.description: power at which thermal compliance should be evaluated at steady-state

peakPower.description: maximum transient power the part will dissipate

values.description: list of values that reflect possible values for a property

siUnit.description: name of SI unit of measure

unitName.description: name of unit if not defined in the siUnit list

typValue.description: typical unit quantity corresponding to unit text - example 40mV would have a value of 40

minValue.description: minimum unit quantity corresponding to unit text - example: 30mV would have a value of 30

maxValue.description: maximum unit quantity corresponding to unit text - example: 50mV would have a value of 50

unitFactor.description: multiplier on the value to achieve the SI unit listed - for example if millivolt was selected, 40mV would have a unitFactor value of 1; if volt was selected, the unitFactor value would be 0.001

relativeValueReference.description: if unit quantity is based on another reference, value of the reference

relativeValueModifier.description: if a unit quantity is based on another reference, the value that edits that reference

relativeValueOperator.description: if a unit quantity is based on another reference, the operation that is performed with the modifier

conditions.description: conditions under which the property is measured

componentID.description: methods for identifying the version of the electronic datasheet

coreProperties.description: core component properties as defined by the specific component spec file

pins.description: array of pin objects with associated properties

thermal.description: component temperature and thermal resistance information

componentPropertyExternalFiles.description: external files that describe key component properties. External files can be used in lieu of defining core properties, pins, and package information in the same file

additionalSpecExternalFiles.description: external files that contain information outside of the json spec. Examples include layout, simulation, etc.

powerSequence.description: . list of power sequence conditions that apply to a component

digitalResolution.description: number of bits of resolution in the digital output

conversionTime.description: time required to convert from an analog signal to digital output

sampleRate.description: maximum rate at which the ADC can convert samples

offsetError.description: difference (in LSB) of the output at the zero point between an actual and ideal ADC

gainError.description: difference (in LSB) of how the actual transfer function matches the ideal transfer function, also called full scale error

integralNonlinearity.description: deviation (in LSB) of an actual transfer function from an ideal transfer function

differentialNonlinearity.description: difference (in LSB) in step width between the actual and ideal transfer functions

rmsNoise.description: root mean square (RMS) noise of ADC

SNR.description: signal to noise (SNR) ratio of the converter

inputType.description: whether the ADC has a single ended or differential input

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes

offsetError.description: analog response to an input code of all zeros
offsetError.$comment: units of percentage of full scale range

gainError.description: difference (in percentage of FSR) of how well the slope of the actual transfer function matches the ideal transfer function
gainError.$comment: units of percentage of full scale range

integralNonlinearity.description: deviation of an actual transfer function from an ideal transfer function, in LSB

differentialNonlinearity.description: difference between the ideal and the actual output responses for successive DAC codes, in LSB

settlingTime.description: time from application of input code to valid output response

sampleRate.description: maximum rate at which the DAC can convert samples

outputType.description: whether the DAC has a single ended or differential output

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes

contactCount.description: number of contacts in a connector

type.description: property describing the method of mating to the connector

cycleRating.description: number of plug/unplug cycles a connector is rated to support

pitch.description: distance from the center of one contact on the connector to the center of the next contact

keying.description: property describing whether a connector has an asymmetry to prevent it from being plugged in the wrong direction

type.description: property describing the way in which the switch is activated

contactType.description: property describing the order in which switch contact is made and broken

circuitConfig.description: property describing the number of poles and throws in a switch

cycleRating.description: number of on/off cycles a mechanical switch can reliably sustain

voltageRating.description: maximum DC voltage potential that can be applied across an open switch

currentRating.description: maximum DC current that can flow through a closed switch without causing excessive heating

onResistance.description: nominal resistance of a closed switch

dielectricRating.description: maximum AC voltage potential that can be applied across an open switch for one minute

inputInterfaces.description: list of interfaces at the input of the bridge

outputInterfaces.description: list of interfaces at the output of the bridge

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes

inputInterfaces.description: list of interfaces high speed mux is designed for

maxDataRate.description: maximum data rate supported by high speed mux

insertionLoss.description: insertion loss through high speed mux

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes

inputVoltage.description: nominal input voltage of level shifter

outputVoltage.description: nominal output voltage of level shifter

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes

numberChannels.description: number of lanes (single ended or differential) supported by redriver

interface.description: list of interface(s) supported by redriver

maxDataRate.description: maximum data rate supported by redriver

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes

numberOfLanes.description: number of lanes (single ended or differential) supported by a device

interface.description: list of interface(s) supported by a device

dpMaxLinkRate.description: maximum interface speed supported by DP interface of a device

maxDataRate.description: maximum data rate supported by a device

integratedAuxLsSwitch.description: whether the AUX/LSx switch for SBU is integrated

commonClock.description: whether a device supports common reference clock

sris.description: whether a device supports Separate Reference clock with Independent Spread spectrum clocking(SRIS)

srns.description: whether a device supports Separate Reference clock with No Spread spectrum clocking (SRNS)

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes

ic_io__usb_bc12.title: specification of USB battery charging 1.2 (bc12) detector

hostMode.description: whether host mode is supported by bc12 chip

deviceMode.description: whether device mode is supported by bc12 chip

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes

pdVersion.description: version of power delivery spec implemented by controller

usbTypecRevision.description: usb type-c spec revision implemented by controller

fastRoleSwapSupport.description: whether the pd controller supports fast role swap (FRS)

vconnPowerSupport.description: whether the pd controller has support for vconn power

vconnPowerLimit.description: power limit supported by internal vconn switch (if supported)

vconnMaxCurrent.description: maximum continuous current supported by internal vconn switch (if supported)

vconnOverCurrentLimit.description: over current limit supported by internal vconn switch (if supported)

integratedVbusDischargeSwitch.description: whether the pd controller has one or more integrated vbus discharge switches

integratedLoadSwitch.description: whether the pd controller has an integrated load switch for vbus power

maxSinkCurrent.description: maximum continuous current supported by pd controller integrated sink load switch

maxSourceCurrent.description: maximum continuous current supported by pd controller integrated source load switch

sinkfetOverCurrentLimit.description: over current limit supported by pd controller integrated sink load switch

sourcefetOverCurrentLimit.description: over current limit supported by pd controller integrated source load switch

onResistanceSinkFet.description: on-resistance of the integrated sink load switch

onResistanceSourceFet.description: on-resistance of the integrated source load switch

interface.description: describes the communication interface from the chip to the host

componentProtectionThresholds.description: thermal and power supply protection thresholds of a device

usbTypecRevision.description: usb type-c spec revision implemented by controller

usbTcpiRevision.description: usb type-c port controller interface spec revision implemented by controller

fastRoleSwapSupport.description: whether the port controller supports fast role swap (FRS)

vconnPowerSupport.description: whether the port controller has support for vconn power

vconnFetOnResistance.description: on-resistance of the integrated Vconn FET

vconnPowerLimit.description: power limit supported by internal vconn switch (if supported)

vconnMaxCurrent.description: maximum continuous current supported by internal vconn switch (if supported)

vconnOverCurrentLimit.description: over current limit supported by internal vconn switch (if supported)

integratedVbusDischargeSwitch.description: whether the port controller has one or more integrated vbus discharge switches

integratedMux.description: whether the port controller has one or more integrated high speed muxes

interface.description: describes the communication interface from the controller to the policy manager

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes

onChipFlash.description: capacity of built-in flash in a microprocessor

onChipRAM.description: capacity of built-in RAM in a microprocessor

onChipROM.description: capacity of built-in ROM in a microprocessor

coreArchitectureBits.description: number of bits of data a CPU can transfer per clock cycle

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes

dataLength.description: number of bits in a data word

hpOutputTHD+N.description: headphone output total harmonic distortion plus noise

micInputTHD+N.description: microphone input total harmonic distortion plus noise

jackDetect.description: describes whether headphone jack detection is supported

outputPower.description: typical output power from speaker amplifier

thd+n.description: typical total harmonic distortion plus noise of amplifier

sampleRate.description: sample rate of data out of amplifier

wlanSpec.description: version of wlan specification supported by module

txrxChains.description: number of tx and rx chains in a wifi module

m2FormFactor.description: wlan module form factor described by jedec standard m.2 form factors

lteCoexFilter.description: describes whether module supports lte coexistence filtering

gpsSupport.description: whether wwan module has gps support

interface.description: describes the communication interface from the chip to the host

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes

type.description: logical operation performed by logic gate

numberGates.description: number of logical gates encapsulated in logic IC

schmittTrigger.description: property describing whether logic gate has schmitt trigger inputs

propagationDelay.description: time between input changing to output changing

rampTime.description: time for output to go from 10% nominal output voltage to 90% nominal output voltage

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes

ranksperModule.description: numbers of ranks on dram module

diesPerChip.description: number of dies on dram chip

channelsPerDie.description: number of channels per die on dram chip

banksPerChannel.description: number of banks per channel of dram

rows.description: number of rows per channel of dram

columns.description: number of columns per row of dram

dataRate.$comment: units of mega transfers per second (MT/s)

latencyCas.description: cl/tCAS, delay between read command issued and first output data available for read
latencyCas.$comment: delay specified by number of clock cycles

delayRasCas.description: tRCD,delay between activation of row and activation of column where data is stored in the dram

delayRasPrecharge.description: tRP, delay between closing access to a row through the precharge command and activating a new row to access data

delayActivePrecharge.description: tRAS, delay between row active command issued and precharge command issued

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes

bitsPerWords.description: number of columns in the eeprom

bootBlockSize.description: size of the eeprom boot block

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes

dataRetention.description: maximum number of read and write cycle the part can support

endurance.description: time in years a bit in the eeprom can retain its data state

numberOfWords.description: number of rows in the eeprom

bootBlockSize.description: size of the flash memory boot block

interface.description: interface of flash memory to host

blockEraseTime.description: time it takes to erase a block (largest erasable unit) of the flash memory

sectorEraseTime.description: time it takes to erase a sector (smallest erasable unit) of the flash memory

chipEraseTime.description: time it takes to erase the flash memory

pageProgramTime.description: time it takes to program a page of the flash memory

endurance.description: time in years a bit in the eeprom can retain its data state

dataRetention.description: maximum number of read and write cycle the part can support

hwReset.description: whether the part supports a hardware reset pin

writeProtect.description: whether the part has a write protect pin

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes

qeStatus.description: indicates whether the Quad Enable(QE) bit is set

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes

ratedVoltage.description: maximum voltage which may be applied continuously to a capacitance

dielectric.description: dielectric material used in the capacitor

polarized.description: describes whether the capacitor is polarized

equivalentSeriesResistance.description: equivalent series resistance (ESR) of the capacitor

temperatureCoefficient.description: change in capacitance when the temperature is changed

minTemperature.description: minimum temperature under which a capacitor can be expected to reliably operate

maxTemperature.description: maximum temperature under which a capacitor can be expected to reliably operate

capacitorDerating.description: graph object to capture capacitance changes with voltage changes

diffModeCutoff.description: frequency at which the differential mode attenuation equals -3dB

commonModeAttenuation.description: graph object to capture common mode attenuation of a common mode choke at various frequencies

dcResistance.description: dc resistance (DCR) of a common mode choke

rmsCurrent.description: applied DC current (IRMS) that produces a common mode choke temperature rise of 40 deg C

intendedApplication.description: intended application of a particular common mode choke

rmsCurrent.description: applied DC current (IRMS) that produces a ferrite bead temperature rise of 40 deg C

impedance100MHz.description: impedance of ferrite bead under standard testing conditions at 100MHz

impedanceTolerance.description: variation of ferrite bead impedance expressed as +/- percentage

ratedCurrent.description: maximum continuous current the inductor can handle

saturationCurrent.description: current where the inductor enters the magnetic state, and the inductance drops a specified amount

rmsCurrent.description: DC current that produces an inductor temperature rise of 40 degrees Celsius

selfResonantFrequency.description: frequency at which the inductor starts behaving like a capacitor

temperatureCoefficient.description: change in inductance when the temperature is changed

minTemperature.description: minimum temperature under which a inductor can be expected to reliably operate

maxTemperature.description: maximum temperature under which a inductor can be expected to reliably operate

saturationCurve.description: graph object to capture inductance as a function of current

resonantFrequencyCurve.description: graph object to capture inductance as a function of frequency

powerRating.description: measure of power a resistor can dissipate indefinitely without degrading performance

temperatureCoefficient.description: change in resistance when the temperature is changed

maxOverloadVoltage.description: maximum voltage that can be applied to the resistor for a short period of time

maxLimitingElementVoltage.description: maximum voltage value that can be applied continuously to the resistor

minTemperature.description: minimum temperature under which a resistor can be expected to reliably operate

maxTemperature.description: maximum temperature under which a resistor can be expected to reliably operate

resistorDerating.description: graph object to capture resistance changes with temperature

chargerTopology.description: type of battery charger topology (Narrow VDC vs Hybrid Power Boost)

batteryConfig.description: battery configuration supported by the device

batteryCellChemistry.description: battery cell chemistry supported by the device

inputPowerSource.description: input power source supported by the device

inputCurrentAccuracy.description: accuracy of input current when set

batteryChargeCurrent.description: charging (fast charge) current of a device

batteryChargeCurrentAccuracy.description: charging current regulation accuracy of a device

batteryPreChargeCurrent.description: charging current of a device in pre-charge phase

batteryTerminationChargeCurrent.description: charging current of a device in charge termination phase

minVinRegulation.description: minimum VIN required to support the max input charger current

batteryTerminationChargeCurrentAccuracy.description: termination charging current regulation accuracy of a device

efficiency.description: charger efficiency as a function of charge current of a device

vin.description: input voltage under which a device can be expected to reliably operate

batteryChargerProtections.description: battery charger specific protections supported by device

integratedLoadSwitch.description: whether the device contains integrated power path load switch(es)

integratedFets.description: whether the device contains integrated switching mosfets

integratedFetProperties.description: describes integrated fet current limits and rdson properties

gateCapacitance.description: describes gate capacitance supported on external fets

passThroughMode.description: whether pass through mode is supported

integratedFetProperties.description: describes integrated fet current limits and rdson properties

dimmingSupport.description: whether a device supports output current dimming

dimmingControl.description: whether a device is dimmed by PWM or I2C

efficiency.description: backlight driver efficiency as a function of forward current

vin.description: minimum input voltage under which the part can be expected to operate without the output dropping

vout.description: output voltage the part can regulate

feedbackVoltage.description: voltage comparison point at the feedback node (vref)

loadCurrent.description: load current supported by a device

currentLimit.description: sustained output current threshold beyond which the output of a device starts drooping

voutAccuracy.description: output voltage variation at no load of a device

loadRegulation.description: output voltage variation,from no load to full load, of a device

lineRegulation.description: output voltage variation,from minimum input voltage to maximum input voltage, of a device

totalOutputNoise.description: total output noise of a device

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes

componentProtectionThresholds.description: thermal and power supply protection thresholds of a device

fetType.description: type of pass FET in a device

loadSwitchCount.description: number of load switches in the package

vin.description: input voltage under which a device can be expected to reliably operate

outputCurrent.description: continuous DC current supported by a device

onResistance.$comment: conditions includes vin and output current

pullDownResistance.description: pull-down resistance of a device from the output to the ground

currentLimitSupport.description: whether a device supports current limiting

adjustableRiseTimeSupport.description: whether a device supports adjustable rise time

quickOutputDischargeSupport.description: whether a device supports quick output discharge

reverseCurrentBlockingSupport.description: whether a device supports reverse current blocking

powerGoodSupport.description: whether a device supports power good

enableTime.description: time between enable asserted and output voltage rising to 10% nominal
enableTime.$comment: conditions include output capacitor value, vin

offTime.description: time between enable deasserted and output voltage falling to 90% nominal
offTime.$comment: conditions include output capacitor value, vin

rampTime.description: time for output voltage to go from 10% vout nominal to 90% vout nominal
rampTime.$comment: conditions include output capacitor value, vin

fallTime.description: time for output voltage to go from 90% vout nominal to 10% vout nominal
fallTime.$comment: conditions include output capacitor value, current output, and vin

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes

componentProtectionThresholds.description: thermal and power supply protection thresholds of a device

instanceSpec.description: array of specifications of instances in the pmic

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes

componentProtectionThresholds.description: thermal and power supply protection thresholds of a device

vin.description: input voltage range under which a device can be expected to reliably operate

vout.description: output voltage a device can regulate

feedbackVoltage.description: voltage comparison point at the feedback node

loadCurrent.description: load current supported by a device

voutAccuracy.description: output voltage variation at no load

loadRegulation.description: output voltage variation from no load to full load

lineRegulation.description: output voltage variation from minimum input voltage to maximum input voltage

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes

enableTime.description: time between enable asserted and output voltage rising to 10% nominal

rampTime.description: time for output voltage to go from 10% vout nominal to 90% vout nominal

integratedFets.description: whether the regulator contains integrated switching mosfets

integratedFetProperties.description: describes integrated fet current limits and rdson properties

componentProtectionThresholds.description: thermal and power supply protection thresholds of a device

efficiency.description: power efficiency of a regulator as a function of output current

bjtChannelType.description: doping of a transistor's channel - describes whether a transistor is n-type or p-type

transistorCount.description: number of transistors in the package

collectorCurrent.description: maximum current flow of BJT as measured at the collector (Icc)

peakCollectorCurrent.description: maximum pulsed current flow of BJT as measured at the collector (Icm)

baseCurrent.description: maximum current flow of BJT as measured at the base (Ib)

peakBaseCurrent.description: maximum pulsed current flow of BJT as measured at the base (Ibm)

collectorBaseVoltage.description: maximum voltage between collector and base terminals of BJT with an open emitter terminal (V_CBO)

collectorEmitterVoltage.description: maximum voltage between collector and emitter terminals of BJT with an open base terminal (V_CEO)

emitterBaseVoltage.description: maximum voltage between emitter and base terminals of BJT with an open collector terminal (V_EBO)

pTot.description: maximum power that can be continuously dissipated under temperature conditions

collectorBaseCutOffCurrent.description: current into the collector terminal when the BJT's base and collector are reverse biased and the emitter is open (I_CBO)

emitterBaseCutOffCurrent.description: current into the base terminal when the BJT's base and emitter are reverse biased and the collector is open (I_EBO)

dcCurrentGain.description: ratio of collector current to base current (hfe)
dcCurrentGain.$comment: conditions include collector current and collector-emitter voltage

collectorEmitterSaturationVoltage.description: collector-emitter voltage below which a change in base current does not impact collector current (VCE_SAT)
collectorEmitterSaturationVoltage.$comment: conditions include collector current and base current

baseEmitterSaturationVoltage.description: base-emitter voltage required to ensure the collector is forward biased for certain current conditions (VBE_SAT)
baseEmitterSaturationVoltage.$comment: conditions include collector current and base current

collectorEmitterBreakdownVoltage.description: collector-emitter voltage at which a specified current flows with the base open

baseEmitterBreakdownVoltage.description: base-emitter voltage at which a specified current flows with the collector open

delayTime.description: time delay between input signal rising and when collector current rises to 10% of Isat (td)
delayTime.$comment: conditions include collector current and base current

riseTime.description: time for collector current to rise through active region from 10% to 90% of Isat (tr)
riseTime.$comment: conditions include collector current and base current

storageTime.description: time delay between input signal falling and when collector current falls to 90% of Isat (ts)
storageTime.$comment: conditions include collector current and base current

fallTime.description: time for collector current to fall from 90% to 10% of Isat (tf)
fallTime.$comment: conditions include collector current and base current

collectorCapacitance.description: parasitic capacitance of collector terminal under certain conditions (Cc)

emitterCapacitance.description: parasitic capacitance of emitter terminal under certain conditions (Ce)

transitionFrequency.description: frequency of unity current gain with a short circuit output (ft)
transitionFrequency.$comment: conditions include collector current, collector-emitter voltage

icVsHfe.description: graph of collector current (Ic) as a function of dc current gain (Hfe)

icVsVce.description: graph of collector current (Ic) as a function of collector-emitter saturation voltage (VCE_SAT)

ibVsVce.description: graph of base current (Ib) as a function of collector-emitter voltage (VCE)

pdVsTemp.description: graph of power dissipation as a function of temperature

diodeCount.description: number of diodes in the package

numberOfProtectedLines.description: number of lines a diode can protect

vf.$comment: conditions include: forward current of diode under which a given forward voltage was measured

if.description: continuous forward current of a diode

ifm.description: maximum continuous forward current a diode can support

ifrm.description: maximum repetitive peak forward current a diode can support

ifsm.description: maximum non-repetitive surge forward current a diode can support

ir.$comment: conditions include: reverse bias voltage under which reverse leakage current occurs

vz.description: breakdown voltage of a zener diode
vz.$comment: conditions include: minimum reverse current condition under which breakdown voltage of a zener diode is achieved

vrm.description: maximum reverse standoff voltage a tvs diode can withstand

vcl.description: clamping voltage of a tvs diode
vcl.$comment: conditions include: surge current condition under which a tvs clamping voltage occurs

vr.description: maximum continuous reverse biased voltage a diode can support

vrrm.description: maximum repetitive reverse voltage pulses a diode can support

cd.description: diode junction capacitance - between the anode and cathode- in reverse bias condition

trr.description: reverse recovery time it takes the diode to stop conducting when its voltage changes from forward-bias to reverse-bias

pTot.description: maximum power dissipation of a forward biased diode

ifVsVf.description: graph of forward current (If) as a function of forward voltage (VfTyp)

vf.$comment: conditions include: forward current of LED under which a given forward voltage was measured

if.description: continuous forward current of an LED

ifp.description: peak forward current of an LED

vr.description: maximum continuous reverse biased voltage an LED can support

ir.$comment: conditions include: reverse biased voltage under which LED ir was measured

iv.$comment: units of candela (cd). Measurement conditions include LED forward current, if

peakWavelength.description: light spectrum output value emitted by an LED at highest wavelength
peakWavelength.$comment: units of nanometer(nm). Measurement conditions include LED forward current, if

dominantWavelength.description: dominant wavelength an LED emits the majority of the time
dominantWavelength.$comment: units of nanometer(nm). Measurement conditions include LED forward current, if

angleHalfIntensity.description: angle at which LED intensity falls to 50% of its maximum value

mosfetChannelType.description: doping of a transistor's channel - describes whether a transistor is n-type or p-type

transistorCount.description: number of transistors in the package

vgs.description: gate to source voltage difference of a MOSFET

vgsMax.description: maximum gate to source voltage difference that can be continuously applied to a MOSFET. This is a limiting value

vds.description: drain to source voltage difference of a MOSFET

vdsMax.description: maximum drain to source voltage difference that can be continuously applied to a MOSFET. This is a limiting value

vdsVbr.description: drain to source breakdown voltage of a MOSFET

vgsTh.description: gate to source voltage difference required to produce a conducting path between drain and source

vsdDiodeVf.description: reverse diode forward voltage when a MOSFET is in off-state

iDrain.description: maximum continuous DC current that can flow through a MOSFET channel.This is a limiting value

idPulsed.description: maximum pulsed DC current that can flow through a MOSFET channel.This is a limiting value

iDss.description: drain-source leakage current of a MOSFET when the gate to source voltage difference is zero

iGss.description: gate-source leakage current of a MOSFET when the drain to source voltage difference is zero

diodeContinuousCurrent.description: maximum continuous forward current of the body diode of a MOSFET (IS).This is a limiting value

diodePulsedCurrent.description: maximum pulsed forward current of the body diode of a MOSFET. This is a limiting value

forwardTransconductance.description: signal gain, change in drain current with variation of gate-source voltage of a MOSFET (gFS)

rg.description: internal gate resistance of a MOSFET

crss.description: reverse transfer capacitance of a MOSFET

qg.description: total gate charge of a MOSFET
qg.$comment: conditions include drain-source voltage and drain current

qgd.description: gate to drain charge of a MOSFET
qgd.$comment: conditions include drain-source voltage and drain current

qgs.description: gate to source charge of a MOSFET
qgs.$comment: conditions include drain-source voltage and drain current

qrr.description: reverse recovery charge of the body diode of a MOSFET

idVsVds.description: graph of drain current (iD) as a function of drain source voltage (vds)

idVsVgs.description: graph of drain current (iD) as a function of gate source voltage (vgs)

tdON.description: turn-on delay time of a MOSFET
tdON.$comment: conditions include drain-source voltage (Vds), gate-source voltage (Vgs), load value.

tdOFF.description: turn-off delay time of a MOSFET
tdOFF.$comment: conditions include drain-source voltage (Vds), gate-source voltage (Vgs), load value.

riseTime.$comment: conditions include drain-source voltage (Vds), gate-source voltage (Vgs), load value.

fallTime.$comment: conditions include drain-source voltage (Vds), gate-source voltage (Vgs), load value.

trr.description: reverse recovery time of the body diode of a MOSFET
trr.$comment: conditions include body diode forward current value and ramp rate.

pTot.description: maximum power dissipation of a MOSFET

pdVsTemp.description: graph of power dissipation vs temperature

accelerationRanges.description: range of force that accelerometer can measure

accelerationSensitivity.description: smallest change in force the accelerometer is able to capture (typical) at a given acceleration range
accelerationSensitivity.$comment: units of LSB/g (least significant bits per g) at a given acceleration range and output resolution

accelerationSensitivityOverTemperature.$comment: units of percentage per degree celsius (%/Celsius)

axis.description: number of axes of acceleration measurement

zerogOffset.description: output of the accelerometer when no acceleration is applied

zerogOffsetOverTemperature.description: accelerometer zero-g offset change over temperature
zerogOffsetOverTemperature.$comment: units of milli g-force per degree celsius (mg/Celsius)

outputDataRate.description: output Data rate (ODR) of a device

rmsNoise.description: broadband rms noise of a device

spectralNoiseDensity.description: spectral noise density of a device
spectralNoiseDensity.$comment: units of milli g-force per square root of Hz (milli-g/rt-Hz)

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes

gyroRanges.description: range of angular speed that gyro can measure

gyroSensitivity.description: smallest change in angular speed gyro is able to capture (typical) at a given gyro range

gyroSensitivityOverTemperature.$comment: units of percentage per degree celsius

zeroRateOffset.description: output of the gyroscope when no angular velocity is applied

zeroRateOffsetOverTemperature.description: gyro zero rate offset change over temperature
zeroRateOffsetOverTemperature.$comment: units of degrees-per-second per degree celsius (dps/Celsius)

outputDataRate.description: output Data rate (ODR) of a device

rmsNoise.description: broadband rms noise of a device

spectralNoiseDensity.description: spectral noise density of a device
spectralNoiseDensity.$comment: units of degrees-per-second per square root Hertz (dps/rt-Hz)

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes

magneticSensingTechnology.description: method by which magnetism is detected

quiescentOutput.description: output of the magnetic sensor when no magnet is present

outputVoltageLinearRange.description: output voltage range over which the magnetic flux density response is linear
outputVoltageLinearRange.$comment: lower value of the range captured by min value and top value of the range captured by max value (V)

linearMagneticSensingRange.description: magnetic flux density range over which the output voltage is linear

sensitivity.description: this is the gain - amount of change in the output voltage for a change in the magnetic flux density

operatePoint.description: magnetic flux density threshold which causes the sensor output to turn on
operatePoint.$comment: units of mT. Depending on the sensor's design, the operation point can cause the output to trip High or Low.

releasePoint.description: magnetic flux density threshold which causes the sensor output to turn off
releasePoint.$comment: units of mT. Depending on the sensor's design, the release point can cause the output to trip High or Low.

outputPolarity.description: indicates whether the sensor output is active high or active low

hysteresis.description: delta between the operate point and the release point threshold

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes

sensingTechnology.description: method by which temperature is detected

gain.description: amount of change in the output voltage for a change in temperature

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes

dataRate.description: maximum data rate of the sd card

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes

type.description: type of ssd storage as defined by interface and technology

dataRate.description: maximum data rate of the ssd

currentConsumption.description: current used by device in various power modes
current used by device in various power modes
